chain.name: spBuildSalesReport
chain.notes: TotalRevenue computed as Qty * UnitPrice (line 498). Two critical branches: Qty from vwConsolidatedSales, UnitPrice from vwPriceList. AdjustedRevenue uses TotalRevenue-Discount (secondary). Other neighbors (CustomerMaster, CustomerSegmentMap, RegionLookup) used only for context/filters, not revenue computation.

chain.name: vwConsolidatedSales
chain.notes: Simple view that renames OrderQty to Qty. DimCalendar used only for filtering (WHERE clause), not contributing to Qty value. SalesStaging is the actual source of OrderQty.

chain.name: vwPriceList
chain.notes: Multi-CTE view (3 renames: ListPrice?BasePrice?AdjPrice?UnitPrice). Pure transformation with 1.0 multiplier (no external source for adjustment). PriceMaster is the single source of ListPrice data.

chain.name: SalesStaging
chain.notes: Staging table with OrderQty. Two upstream procedures: spArchiveOldOrders (likely removes old rows) and spLoadSalesStaging (likely loads fresh data). Need to check both for OrderQty contribution.

chain.name: PriceMaster
chain.summary: Source of ListPrice, which becomes UnitPrice through CTE transforms
chain.notes: Source table for ListPrice (Qty is independent). PriceMaster is maintained by spRefreshPrices.

chain.name: spArchiveOldOrders
chain.summary: Check if this procedure writes/modifies OrderQty in SalesStaging
chain.notes: Reads OrderQty from SalesStaging and archives to ArchiveOrders, then deletes from SalesStaging. This is data maintenance/lifecycle, not source. Does not CREATE OrderQty. Cycle detected: reads what already exists.

chain.name: spLoadSalesStaging
chain.summary: Check if this procedure writes/modifies OrderQty in SalesStaging
chain.notes: TRUNCATE+INSERT loader. Selects OrderQty from vwRawOrders and inserts into SalesStaging. DimCalendar used only for UPDATE (DateKey backfill), not for OrderQty value. vwRawOrders is the actual source.

chain.name: spRefreshPrices
chain.summary: Procedure that inserts/updates ListPrice in PriceMaster
chain.notes: ListPrice = CostPrice * (1 + MarkupPct). Two critical inputs: CostPrice from SupplierPrices, MarkupPct from MarkupRules. RegionLookup (CROSS JOIN source) and CurrencyConfig (filter-only) are not part of ListPrice computation.

chain.name: vwRawOrders
chain.summary: Source view for OrderQty and other staging columns
chain.notes: Simple SELECT * passthrough view with IsValid filter. CleanedOrders is the single source of all columns including OrderQty.

chain.name: SupplierPrices
chain.summary: Source of CostPrice in ListPrice formula

chain.name: MarkupRules
chain.summary: Source of MarkupPct in ListPrice formula

chain.name: CleanedOrders
chain.summary: Source table of OrderQty (passthrough via SELECT *)
chain.notes: Staging table for cleaned orders with OrderQty. Fed by spCleanOrders.

chain.name: spCleanOrders
chain.summary: Procedure that cleans/validates order data and populates CleanedOrders
chain.notes: Cleaning/dedup procedure. RawQty from RawOrderImport is renamed to OrderQty via COALESCE. CustomerMaster is used only for enrichment (JOIN for CustomerName), not for Qty value. CleanedOrders is the target table.

chain.name: RawOrderImport
chain.notes: Raw import table with RawQty. Fed by spImportOrders from external source.

chain.name: spImportOrders
chain.notes: Import procedure from external sources. Renames Quantity?RawQty. ActiveRegions is filter-only (WHERE clause). vwExternalOrders is the actual data source.

chain.name: vwExternalOrders
chain.summary: External orders view with Quantity (renamed to RawQty)
chain.notes: UNION view with two quantity sources: SAP has Quantity column directly, Oracle has ItemCount renamed to Quantity. Both are external data sources (source boundaries).

chain.name: SAPOrders
chain.summary: SAP external system - Quantity flows directly

chain.name: OracleOrders
chain.summary: Oracle external system - ItemCount renamed to Quantity

fullNodes.ddl:
-- Level 2a: renames OrderQty→Qty, joins DimCalendar for filter ONLY
CREATE VIEW [ai].[vwConsolidatedSales]
AS
    SELECT
        s.StagingID,
        s.OrderQty         AS Qty,
        s.OrderAmount,
        s.RegionCode,
        s.OrderDate,
        s.CustomerID,
        s.CustomerName
    FROM [ai].[SalesStaging] s
    -- DimCalendar is a FILTER-ONLY join: no columns selected from dc
    INNER JOIN [ai].[DimCalendar] dc
        ON s.OrderDateKey = dc.DateKey
    WHERE dc.FiscalYear = YEAR(GETDATE())
      AND dc.IsBusinessDay = 1

-- Level 2b: multi-CTE view with 3 renames (ListPrice→BasePrice→AdjPrice→UnitPrice)
CREATE VIEW [ai].[vwPriceList]
AS
    WITH CTE_Raw AS (
        -- Stage 1: raw prices from PriceMaster
        SELECT
            ProductCode,
            RegionCode,
            ListPrice        AS BasePrice,
            EffectiveFrom,
            EffectiveTo
        FROM [ai].[PriceMaster]
        WHERE EffectiveTo IS NULL   -- only current prices
    ),
    CTE_Adjusted AS (
        -- Stage 2: apply seasonal adjustment factor (1.0 = no change)
        -- NOTE: the adjustment is a fixed multiplier here, no extra table
        SELECT
            ProductCode,
            RegionCode,
            BasePrice * 1.0  AS AdjPrice,
            EffectiveFrom
        FROM CTE_Raw
    ),
    CTE_Final AS (
        -- Stage 3: final rename to UnitPrice
        -- v1 had this as NetPrice but was renamed in Q3 2025
        SELECT
            ProductCode,
            RegionCode,
            AdjPrice         AS UnitPrice,
            EffectiveFrom
        FROM CTE_Adjusted
    )
    SELECT
        ProductCode,
        RegionCode,
        UnitPrice,
        EffectiveFrom
    FROM CTE_Final

-- ─── spRefreshPrices (Branch B, Level 4b) ───────────────────────────────────
-- Loads PriceMaster from SupplierPrices + MarkupRules
-- CurrencyConfig is a FILTER-ONLY join (no columns flow to ListPrice)
CREATE PROCEDURE [ai].[spRefreshPrices]
AS
BEGIN
    SET NOCOUNT ON;
    -- Step 1: Load raw supplier costs into temp
    -- NOTE: CurrencyConfig is only used for filtering, NOT for price calculation
    SELECT
        sp.ProductCode,
        sp.CostPrice,
        sp.Currency,
        sp.EffectiveFrom,
        r.RegionCode
    INTO #SupplierCosts
    FROM [ai].[SupplierPrices] sp
    CROSS JOIN [ai].[RegionLookup] r
    -- CurrencyConfig: FILTER ONLY — controls which currencies we accept
    INNER JOIN [ai].[CurrencyConfig] cc
        ON sp.Currency = cc.Code
    WHERE cc.IsBase = 1
      AND sp.EffectiveTo IS NULL;
    -- Step 2: Apply markup rules to compute ListPrice
    -- TODO: also pull TotalRevenue from ai.LegacyFacts (table doesn't exist, old note)
    SELECT
        sc.ProductCode,
        sc.RegionCode,
        sc.CostPrice * (1 + COALESCE(m.MarkupPct, 0.15)) AS ListPrice,
        sc.EffectiveFrom
    INTO #PricedItems
    FROM #SupplierCosts sc
    LEFT JOIN [ai].[MarkupRules] m
        ON sc.RegionCode = m.RegionCode
       AND m.ValidTo IS NULL;
    /* Old approach: SELECT TotalRevenue FROM ai.SalesStaging
       This was the v1 logic that pulled prices from sales data.
       Removed in Q2 2025 when we switched to supplier-based pricing.
       SELECT s.OrderAmount AS ListPrice, s.RegionCode
       FROM ai.SalesStaging s
       WHERE s.IsValid = 1
    */
    -- Step 3: Upsert into PriceMaster
    MERGE [ai].[PriceMaster] AS tgt
    USING #PricedItems AS src
        ON  tgt.ProductCode   = src.ProductCode
        AND tgt.RegionCode    = src.RegionCode
        AND tgt.EffectiveFrom = src.EffectiveFrom
    WHEN MATCHED THEN
        UPDATE SET
            tgt.ListPrice     = src.ListPrice,
            tgt.LastRefreshed = SYSUTCDATETIME()
    WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN
        INSERT (ProductCode, RegionCode, ListPrice, EffectiveFrom, LastRefreshed)
        VALUES (src.ProductCode, src.RegionCode, src.ListPrice, src.EffectiveFrom, SYSUTCDATETIME());
    -- Cleanup
    DROP TABLE #SupplierCosts;
    DROP TABLE #PricedItems;
    EXEC [ai].[spLogAudit]
        @EventType  = 'REFRESH',
        @ObjectName = 'PriceMaster',
        @AffectedRows = @@ROWCOUNT;
END;

-- Level 5: SELECT * passthrough with filter
CREATE VIEW [ai].[vwRawOrders]
AS
    -- Simple passthrough: all columns flow through, only rows are filtered
    SELECT *
    FROM [ai].[CleanedOrders]
    WHERE IsValid = 1

-- ─── spCleanOrders (Level 7 — multi-temp, CTE, COALESCE, EXEC dead end) ────
-- Renames: RawQty→OrderQty (via COALESCE), RawAmount→OrderAmount
-- CustomerMaster join enriches CustomerName (dead end for Qty trace)
-- EXEC spLogAudit = dead end (audit, no data columns)
CREATE PROCEDURE [ai].[spCleanOrders]
    @BatchFilter     INT          = NULL,  -- NULL = all batches
    @MaxAgeHours     INT          = 168,   -- only clean orders < 7 days old
    @DedupStrategy   VARCHAR(20)  = 'LATEST', -- LATEST / FIRST / SUM
    @ValidateAmounts BIT          = 1,
    @DebugLevel      INT          = 0
AS
BEGIN
    /*
    ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    spCleanOrders — Deduplication, enrichment, and quality control pipeline
    COLUMN FLOW (the only thing that matters for lineage):
    ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    RawOrderImport.RawQty    → COALESCE(r.RawQty, 0) AS OrderQty → #Deduped.OrderQty
    RawOrderImport.RawAmount → COALESCE(r.RawAmount, 0) AS OrderAmount → #Deduped.OrderAmount
    #Deduped → #Enriched (adds CustomerName from CustomerMaster, dead end for Qty)
    #Enriched.OrderQty → CleanedOrders.OrderQty (passthrough)
    #Enriched.OrderAmount → CleanedOrders.OrderAmount (passthrough)
    DEAD ENDS:
    ──────────
    - CustomerMaster: joined for CustomerName enrichment only, does NOT feed OrderQty
    - spLogAudit: EXEC call for audit trail, no data columns flow
    - TierCode: computed from CustomerMaster.CustomerTier, used for stats only
    VERSION HISTORY:
    ────────────────
    v1.0 (2024-Q1) Simple INSERT...SELECT with dedup CTE
    v1.1 (2024-Q2) Added CustomerMaster enrichment for downstream segment analysis
    v2.0 (2024-Q3) Refactored to multi-temp table approach for better error isolation
    v2.1 (2024-Q4) Added @DedupStrategy parameter (LATEST vs FIRST vs SUM)
    v3.0 (2025-Q1) Added data quality validation steps (amounts, dates, regions)
    v3.1 (2025-Q2) Added statistical profiling for monitoring dashboard
    v3.2 (2025-Q3) Added @DebugLevel parameter, concurrency control
    v4.0 (2025-Q4) Performance: added index hints for large batch processing
    v4.1 (2026-Q1) Fixed edge case: NULL CustomerRef causing cross-join explosion
                   (Bug #4782: CustomerMaster LEFT JOIN produced cartesian when CustomerRef IS NULL)
    ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    */
    SET NOCOUNT ON;
    SET XACT_ABORT OFF;
    -- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    -- INITIALIZATION
    -- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    DECLARE @StartTime       DATETIME2 = SYSUTCDATETIME();
    DECLARE @StepStart       DATETIME2;
    DECLARE @RowsBefore      INT = 0;
    DECLARE @RowsAfter       INT = 0;
    DECLARE @DuplicateCount  INT = 0;
    DECLARE @InvalidCount    INT = 0;
    DECLARE @EnrichedCount   INT = 0;
    DECLARE @WarningCount    INT = 0;
    DECLARE @ErrorCount      INT = 0;
    DECLARE @CurrentStep     VARCHAR(50);
    DECLARE @StatusMsg       NVARCHAR(MAX) = N'';
    DECLARE @CutoffTime      DATETIME2 = DATEADD(HOUR, -@MaxAgeHours, SYSUTCDATETIME());
    -- Validate parameters
    IF @DedupStrategy NOT IN ('LATEST', 'FIRST', 'SUM')
    BEGIN
        INSERT INTO [ai].[ErrorLog] (ProcedureName, ErrorNumber, ErrorMessage)
        VALUES ('spCleanOrders.Validate', 50020,
            'Invalid @DedupStrategy: ' + ISNULL(@DedupStrategy, 'NULL'));
        RETURN;
    END;
    IF @MaxAgeHours < 1 OR @MaxAgeHours > 8760
    BEGIN
        SET @MaxAgeHours = 168;
        SET @WarningCount = @WarningCount + 1;
    END;
    -- Concurrency control
    DECLARE @LockResult INT;
    EXEC @LockResult = sp_getapplock
        @Resource   = 'spCleanOrders_Lock',
        @LockMode   = 'Exclusive',
        @LockOwner  = 'Session',
        @LockTimeout = 10000;
    IF @LockResult < 0
    BEGIN
        INSERT INTO [ai].[ErrorLog] (ProcedureName, ErrorNumber, ErrorMessage)
        VALUES ('spCleanOrders.Lock', 50021,
            'Could not acquire lock. Result=' + CAST(@LockResult AS VARCHAR));
        RETURN;
    END;
    IF @DebugLevel >= 1
    BEGIN
        DECLARE @DebugMsg NVARCHAR(500) = CONCAT(
            N'[spCleanOrders] Starting. Batch=', ISNULL(CAST(@BatchFilter AS NVARCHAR), 'ALL'),
            N', MaxAge=', @MaxAgeHours, N'h, Dedup=', @DedupStrategy,
            N', ValidateAmounts=', @ValidateAmounts);
        RAISERROR(@DebugMsg, 0, 1) WITH NOWAIT;
    END;
    -- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    -- STEP 1: Pre-clean profiling — collect stats on raw input data
    -- This helps identify data quality trends over time.
    -- None of these stats affect the actual data flow.
    -- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    SET @CurrentStep = 'Step1_Profile';
    SET @StepStart = SYSUTCDATETIME();
    DECLARE @InputProfile TABLE (
        Metric        VARCHAR(50),
        NumericValue  DECIMAL(18,2),
        TextValue     VARCHAR(200)
    );
    INSERT INTO @InputProfile
    SELECT 'TotalRawRows', COUNT(*), NULL
    FROM [ai].[RawOrderImport]
    WHERE (@BatchFilter IS NULL OR BatchID = @BatchFilter)
      AND ImportedAt >= @CutoffTime
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'AvgRawQty', AVG(CAST(RawQty AS DECIMAL(18,2))), NULL
    FROM [ai].[RawOrderImport]
    WHERE (@BatchFilter IS NULL OR BatchID = @BatchFilter)
      AND ImportedAt >= @CutoffTime
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'MaxRawQty', MAX(CAST(RawQty AS DECIMAL(18,2))), NULL
    FROM [ai].[RawOrderImport]
    WHERE (@BatchFilter IS NULL OR BatchID = @BatchFilter)
      AND ImportedAt >= @CutoffTime
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'NullQtyCount', SUM(CASE WHEN RawQty IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), NULL
    FROM [ai].[RawOrderImport]
    WHERE (@BatchFilter IS NULL OR BatchID = @BatchFilter)
      AND ImportedAt >= @CutoffTime
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'NegativeQtyCount', SUM(CASE WHEN RawQty < 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), NULL
    FROM [ai].[RawOrderImport]
    WHERE (@BatchFilter IS NULL OR BatchID = @BatchFilter)
      AND ImportedAt >= @CutoffTime
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'DistinctRegions', COUNT(DISTINCT RegionCode), NULL
    FROM [ai].[RawOrderImport]
    WHERE (@BatchFilter IS NULL OR BatchID = @BatchFilter)
      AND ImportedAt >= @CutoffTime
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'DistinctSources', COUNT(DISTINCT SourceSystem), NULL
    FROM [ai].[RawOrderImport]
    WHERE (@BatchFilter IS NULL OR BatchID = @BatchFilter)
      AND ImportedAt >= @CutoffTime
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'DateRange', DATEDIFF(DAY, MIN(OrderDate), MAX(OrderDate)),
           CONCAT(FORMAT(MIN(OrderDate), 'yyyy-MM-dd'), ' to ', FORMAT(MAX(OrderDate), 'yyyy-MM-dd'))
    FROM [ai].[RawOrderImport]
    WHERE (@BatchFilter IS NULL OR BatchID = @BatchFilter)
      AND ImportedAt >= @CutoffTime;
    IF @DebugLevel >= 2
        SELECT * FROM @InputProfile;
    -- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    -- STEP 2: Deduplicate raw orders into #Deduped
    -- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    -- Uses CTE with ROW_NUMBER for deduplication.
    -- The COLUMN RENAME happens here:
    --   RawOrderImport.RawQty    → COALESCE(r.RawQty, 0) AS OrderQty
    --   RawOrderImport.RawAmount → COALESCE(r.RawAmount, 0) AS OrderAmount
    -- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    SET @CurrentStep = 'Step2_Dedup';
    SET @StepStart = SYSUTCDATETIME();
    -- Pre-create #Deduped (required: SQL Server disallows multiple SELECT INTO for same temp table)
    CREATE TABLE #Deduped (
        ImportID       BIGINT,
        OrderQty       INT           NOT NULL,
        OrderAmount    DECIMAL(18,2) NOT NULL,
        RegionCode     VARCHAR(10)   NOT NULL,
        OrderDate      DATE          NOT NULL,
        CustomerRef    VARCHAR(50)   NULL,
        SourceSystem   VARCHAR(10)   NULL,
        IsValid        BIT           NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
        ValidationMsg  NVARCHAR(500) NULL
    );
    BEGIN TRY
        IF @DedupStrategy = 'LATEST'
        BEGIN
            ;WITH CTE_Ranked AS (
                SELECT
                    r.ImportID,
                    COALESCE(r.RawQty, 0)     AS OrderQty,
                    COALESCE(r.RawAmount, 0)  AS OrderAmount,
                    r.RegionCode,
                    r.OrderDate,
                    r.CustomerRef,
                    r.SourceSystem,
                    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
                        PARTITION BY r.RegionCode, r.OrderDate, r.RawQty
                        ORDER BY r.ImportedAt DESC
                    ) AS DupRank
                FROM [ai].[RawOrderImport] r
                WHERE r.BatchID IS NOT NULL
                  AND (@BatchFilter IS NULL OR r.BatchID = @BatchFilter)
                  AND r.ImportedAt >= @CutoffTime
            )
            INSERT INTO #Deduped (ImportID, OrderQty, OrderAmount, RegionCode, OrderDate, CustomerRef, SourceSystem)
            SELECT ImportID, OrderQty, OrderAmount, RegionCode, OrderDate, CustomerRef, SourceSystem
            FROM CTE_Ranked
            WHERE DupRank = 1;
        END
        ELSE IF @DedupStrategy = 'FIRST'
        BEGIN
            ;WITH CTE_Ranked AS (
                SELECT
                    r.ImportID,
                    COALESCE(r.RawQty, 0)     AS OrderQty,
                    COALESCE(r.RawAmount, 0)  AS OrderAmount,
                    r.RegionCode,
                    r.OrderDate,
                    r.CustomerRef,
                    r.SourceSystem,
                    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
                        PARTITION BY r.RegionCode, r.OrderDate, r.RawQty
                        ORDER BY r.ImportedAt ASC  -- FIRST instead of LATEST
                    ) AS DupRank
                FROM [ai].[RawOrderImport] r
                WHERE r.BatchID IS NOT NULL
                  AND (@BatchFilter IS NULL OR r.BatchID = @BatchFilter)
                  AND r.ImportedAt >= @CutoffTime
            )
            INSERT INTO #Deduped (ImportID, OrderQty, OrderAmount, RegionCode, OrderDate, CustomerRef, SourceSystem)
            SELECT ImportID, OrderQty, OrderAmount, RegionCode, OrderDate, CustomerRef, SourceSystem
            FROM CTE_Ranked
            WHERE DupRank = 1;
        END
        ELSE  -- SUM strategy: aggregate duplicates
        BEGIN
            INSERT INTO #Deduped (ImportID, OrderQty, OrderAmount, RegionCode, OrderDate, CustomerRef, SourceSystem)
            SELECT
                MIN(r.ImportID),
                COALESCE(SUM(r.RawQty), 0),
                COALESCE(SUM(r.RawAmount), 0),
                r.RegionCode,
                r.OrderDate,
                MIN(r.CustomerRef),
                MIN(r.SourceSystem)
            FROM [ai].[RawOrderImport] r
            WHERE r.BatchID IS NOT NULL
              AND (@BatchFilter IS NULL OR r.BatchID = @BatchFilter)
              AND r.ImportedAt >= @CutoffTime
            GROUP BY r.RegionCode, r.OrderDate;
        END;
        SET @RowsBefore = @@ROWCOUNT;
        -- Count duplicates that were eliminated
        SELECT @DuplicateCount = COUNT(*)
        FROM [ai].[RawOrderImport] r
        WHERE r.BatchID IS NOT NULL
          AND (@BatchFilter IS NULL OR r.BatchID = @BatchFilter)
          AND r.ImportedAt >= @CutoffTime;
        SET @DuplicateCount = @DuplicateCount - @RowsBefore;
        IF @DebugLevel >= 1
            RAISERROR('[Step2] Deduplication: %d rows (eliminated %d dupes)', 0, 1, @RowsBefore, @DuplicateCount) WITH NOWAIT;
    END TRY
    BEGIN CATCH
        INSERT INTO [ai].[ErrorLog] (ProcedureName, ErrorNumber, ErrorMessage, ErrorLine)
        VALUES ('spCleanOrders.Step2', ERROR_NUMBER(), ERROR_MESSAGE(), ERROR_LINE());
        SET @ErrorCount = @ErrorCount + 1;
        GOTO CleanupClean;
    END CATCH;
    /* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
       OLD DEDUP APPROACH (pre v2.0):
       Used DELETE with self-join instead of CTE + ROW_NUMBER.
       This was O(n^2) and caused tempdb spills on batches > 50K rows.
       DELETE d1
       FROM [ai].[RawOrderImport] d1
       INNER JOIN [ai].[RawOrderImport] d2
           ON  d1.RegionCode = d2.RegionCode
           AND d1.OrderDate  = d2.OrderDate
           AND d1.RawQty     = d2.RawQty
           AND d1.ImportID   > d2.ImportID;
       -- v1.5 tried using MERGE for dedup:
       -- MERGE [ai].[CleanedOrders] AS tgt
       -- USING (SELECT DISTINCT RawQty AS OrderQty, RawAmount AS OrderAmount, ...
       --        FROM [ai].[RawOrderImport]) AS src
       -- ON tgt.OrderDate = src.OrderDate AND tgt.RegionCode = src.RegionCode
       -- WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT ...
       -- WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET tgt.OrderQty = src.OrderQty
       -- This was abandoned because MERGE has well-known bugs with parallel plans
    ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */
    -- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    -- STEP 3: Data quality validation on #Deduped
    -- Validates amounts, dates, and region codes.
    -- Does NOT change OrderQty or OrderAmount values — only flags rows.
    -- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    SET @CurrentStep = 'Step3_Validate';
    SET @StepStart = SYSUTCDATETIME();
    IF @ValidateAmounts = 1
    BEGIN
        -- 3a: Flag zero-qty AND zero-amount (completely empty orders)
        UPDATE #Deduped
        SET ValidationMsg = 'Empty order: zero qty and zero amount',
            IsValid = 0
        WHERE OrderQty = 0 AND OrderAmount = 0;
        -- 3b: Flag negative amounts
        UPDATE #Deduped
        SET ValidationMsg = COALESCE(ValidationMsg + '; ', '') + 'Negative amount: ' + CAST(OrderAmount AS VARCHAR),
            IsValid = 0
        WHERE OrderAmount < 0;
        -- 3c: Flag outlier amounts (> 5M per order)
        UPDATE #Deduped
        SET ValidationMsg = COALESCE(ValidationMsg + '; ', '') + 'Outlier amount: ' + CAST(OrderAmount AS VARCHAR)
        WHERE OrderAmount > 5000000;
        -- 3d: Flag future dates
        UPDATE #Deduped
        SET ValidationMsg = COALESCE(ValidationMsg + '; ', '') + 'Future date: ' + FORMAT(OrderDate, 'yyyy-MM-dd')
        WHERE OrderDate > DATEADD(DAY, 1, GETDATE());
        -- 3e: Flag very old dates (> 5 years)
        UPDATE #Deduped
        SET ValidationMsg = COALESCE(ValidationMsg + '; ', '') + 'Old date: ' + FORMAT(OrderDate, 'yyyy-MM-dd')
        WHERE OrderDate < DATEADD(YEAR, -5, GETDATE());
        -- Mark remaining rows as valid
        UPDATE #Deduped SET IsValid = 1 WHERE IsValid = 0 AND ValidationMsg IS NULL;
        -- Actually all rows without validation messages are valid
        UPDATE #Deduped SET IsValid = 1 WHERE ValidationMsg IS NULL;
        SELECT @InvalidCount = COUNT(*) FROM #Deduped WHERE IsValid = 0;
        IF @DebugLevel >= 1
            RAISERROR('[Step3] Validation: %d invalid rows flagged', 0, 1, @InvalidCount) WITH NOWAIT;
    END
    ELSE
    BEGIN
        -- Skip validation: mark all as valid
        UPDATE #Deduped SET IsValid = 1;
    END;
    -- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    -- STEP 4: Enrich with customer data into #Enriched
    -- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    -- CustomerMaster provides CustomerName (NOT relevant to OrderQty/OrderAmount)
    -- CustomerID is resolved from CustomerRef by casting to INT
    --
    -- IMPORTANT: CustomerMaster is a DEAD END for column lineage.
    -- Only CustomerName and TierCode come from it. Neither affects OrderQty.
    -- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    SET @CurrentStep = 'Step4_Enrich';
    SET @StepStart = SYSUTCDATETIME();
    BEGIN TRY
        SELECT
            d.ImportID,
            d.OrderQty,
            d.OrderAmount,
            d.RegionCode,
            d.OrderDate,
            d.SourceSystem,
            cm.CustomerID,
            cm.CustomerName,
            -- CustomerMaster.CustomerTier feeds segment logic, NOT revenue columns
            CASE
                WHEN cm.CustomerTier = 'Premium'  THEN 1
                WHEN cm.CustomerTier = 'Standard' THEN 0
                ELSE -1
            END AS TierCode,
            d.IsValid,
            d.ValidationMsg,
            CASE
                WHEN cm.CustomerID IS NOT NULL THEN 'matched'
                WHEN d.CustomerRef IS NOT NULL THEN 'unmatched'
                ELSE 'anonymous'
            END AS CustomerMatchStatus
        INTO #Enriched
        FROM #Deduped d
        LEFT JOIN [ai].[CustomerMaster] cm
            ON d.CustomerRef = CAST(cm.CustomerID AS VARCHAR(50))
           AND cm.IsActive = 1;
        SET @EnrichedCount = @@ROWCOUNT;
        /* Old enrichment approach (pre v4.1):
           This used a subquery that caused cartesian explosion when CustomerRef was NULL:
           LEFT JOIN [ai].[CustomerMaster] cm ON cm.CustomerID = TRY_CAST(d.CustomerRef AS INT)
           Bug #4782: when CustomerRef = NULL, TRY_CAST returns NULL which matches all NULL CustomerIDs
        */
        IF @DebugLevel >= 1
        BEGIN
            DECLARE @MatchedCount INT, @UnmatchedCount INT, @AnonCount INT;
            SELECT @MatchedCount = SUM(CASE WHEN CustomerMatchStatus = 'matched' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),
                   @UnmatchedCount = SUM(CASE WHEN CustomerMatchStatus = 'unmatched' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),
                   @AnonCount = SUM(CASE WHEN CustomerMatchStatus = 'anonymous' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
            FROM #Enriched;
            RAISERROR('[Step4] Enrichment: %d matched, %d unmatched, %d anonymous', 0, 1,
                @MatchedCount, @UnmatchedCount, @AnonCount) WITH NOWAIT;
        END;
        IF @DebugLevel >= 2
        BEGIN
            -- Tier distribution (for monitoring dashboard)
            SELECT
                CASE TierCode WHEN 1 THEN 'Premium' WHEN 0 THEN 'Standard' ELSE 'Other' END AS Tier,
                COUNT(*) AS [RowCount],
                SUM(OrderQty) AS TotalQty,
                SUM(OrderAmount) AS TotalAmount,
                AVG(OrderAmount) AS AvgAmount
            FROM #Enriched
            GROUP BY TierCode
            ORDER BY TierCode;
            -- Source system breakdown
            SELECT
                SourceSystem,
                COUNT(*) AS [RowCount],
                SUM(OrderQty) AS TotalQty,
                SUM(OrderAmount) AS TotalAmount
            FROM #Enriched
            GROUP BY SourceSystem;
        END;
    END TRY
    BEGIN CATCH
        INSERT INTO [ai].[ErrorLog] (ProcedureName, ErrorNumber, ErrorMessage, ErrorLine)
        VALUES ('spCleanOrders.Step4', ERROR_NUMBER(), ERROR_MESSAGE(), ERROR_LINE());
        SET @ErrorCount = @ErrorCount + 1;
        GOTO CleanupClean;
    END CATCH;
    -- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    -- STEP 5: Insert into CleanedOrders from #Enriched
    -- This is the ACTUAL data flow for OrderQty and OrderAmount
    -- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    SET @CurrentStep = 'Step5_Insert';
    SET @StepStart = SYSUTCDATETIME();
    BEGIN TRY
        INSERT INTO [ai].[CleanedOrders]
            (OrderQty, OrderAmount, RegionCode, OrderDate, CustomerID, CustomerName, IsValid)
        SELECT
            e.OrderQty,
            e.OrderAmount,
            e.RegionCode,
            e.OrderDate,
            e.CustomerID,
            e.CustomerName,
            CASE
                WHEN e.IsValid = 0                              THEN 0
                WHEN e.OrderQty > 0 AND e.OrderAmount > 0      THEN 1
                WHEN e.OrderQty = 0 AND e.OrderAmount > 0      THEN 1  -- zero-qty but has amount
                WHEN e.OrderQty > 0 AND e.OrderAmount = 0      THEN 1  -- has qty, free items
                ELSE 0
            END AS IsValid
        FROM #Enriched e
        WHERE e.IsValid = 1;  -- only insert validated rows
        SET @RowsAfter = @@ROWCOUNT;
        IF @DebugLevel >= 1
            RAISERROR('[Step5] Inserted %d rows into CleanedOrders', 0, 1, @RowsAfter) WITH NOWAIT;
    END TRY
    BEGIN CATCH
        INSERT INTO [ai].[ErrorLog] (ProcedureName, ErrorNumber, ErrorMessage, ErrorLine)
        VALUES ('spCleanOrders.Step5', ERROR_NUMBER(), ERROR_MESSAGE(), ERROR_LINE());
        SET @ErrorCount = @ErrorCount + 1;
    END CATCH;
    -- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    -- STEP 6: Post-insert statistics
    -- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    SET @CurrentStep = 'Step6_PostStats';
    DECLARE @PostStats TABLE (
        RegionCode VARCHAR(10),
        InsertedRows INT,
        TotalQty BIGINT,
        TotalAmount DECIMAL(18,2),
        AvgQty DECIMAL(18,2),
        MaxQty INT
    );
    INSERT INTO @PostStats
    SELECT
        RegionCode,
        COUNT(*),
        SUM(CAST(OrderQty AS BIGINT)),
        SUM(OrderAmount),
        AVG(CAST(OrderQty AS DECIMAL(18,2))),
        MAX(OrderQty)
    FROM [ai].[CleanedOrders]
    WHERE CleanedAt >= @StartTime
    GROUP BY RegionCode;
    IF @DebugLevel >= 2
        SELECT * FROM @PostStats ORDER BY RegionCode;
    /* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
       DATA LINEAGE VERIFICATION QUERY (for manual audit)
       Traces the column rename from RawQty to OrderQty:
       SELECT
           roi.ImportID,
           roi.RawQty        AS 'RawOrderImport.RawQty',
           co.OrderQty       AS 'CleanedOrders.OrderQty',
           CASE WHEN COALESCE(roi.RawQty, 0) = co.OrderQty THEN 'MATCH' ELSE 'MISMATCH' END AS QtyCheck,
           roi.RawAmount     AS 'RawOrderImport.RawAmount',
           co.OrderAmount    AS 'CleanedOrders.OrderAmount',
           CASE WHEN COALESCE(roi.RawAmount, 0) = co.OrderAmount THEN 'MATCH' ELSE 'MISMATCH' END AS AmtCheck
       FROM [ai].[RawOrderImport] roi
       INNER JOIN [ai].[CleanedOrders] co
           ON roi.OrderDate = co.OrderDate
          AND roi.RegionCode = co.RegionCode
          AND COALESCE(roi.RawQty, 0) = co.OrderQty
       WHERE roi.ImportedAt >= DATEADD(HOUR, -1, GETDATE())
       ORDER BY roi.ImportedAt DESC;
       -- Expected: RawQty should equal OrderQty (after COALESCE of NULLs to 0)
       -- Any MISMATCH indicates a bug in the cleaning pipeline
    ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */
    -- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    -- CLEANUP: Drop temp tables, release lock, audit
    -- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    CleanupClean:
    DROP TABLE IF EXISTS #Deduped;
    DROP TABLE IF EXISTS #Enriched;
    DECLARE @Duration INT = DATEDIFF(MILLISECOND, @StartTime, SYSUTCDATETIME());
    SET @StatusMsg = CONCAT(
        N'Clean: ', @RowsAfter, N'/', @RowsBefore, N' rows',
        N' (', @DuplicateCount, N' dupes, ', @InvalidCount, N' invalid)',
        N' Strategy=', @DedupStrategy,
        N' in ', @Duration, N'ms',
        CASE WHEN @WarningCount > 0 THEN CONCAT(N' [', @WarningCount, N' warnings]') ELSE N'' END,
        CASE WHEN @ErrorCount > 0 THEN CONCAT(N' [', @ErrorCount, N' errors]') ELSE N'' END
    );
    -- Audit (dead end: no data columns flow through this EXEC)
    EXEC [ai].[spLogAudit]
        @EventType    = 'CLEAN',
        @ObjectName   = 'CleanedOrders',
        @AffectedRows = @RowsAfter,
        @Message      = @StatusMsg;
    -- Release lock
    EXEC sp_releaseapplock @Resource = 'spCleanOrders_Lock', @LockOwner = 'Session';
    IF @DebugLevel >= 1
        RAISERROR('[Complete] %d rows cleaned in %dms', 0, 1, @RowsAfter, @Duration) WITH NOWAIT;
END;

-- ============================================================================
-- VIEWS
-- ============================================================================
-- Level 10: UNION ALL with rename (ItemCount→Quantity from Oracle)
CREATE VIEW [ai].[vwExternalOrders]
AS
    -- SAP source: columns map directly
    SELECT
        OrderID,
        Quantity,
        Amount,
        RegionCode,
        OrderDate,
        'SAP' AS SourceSystem
    FROM [ai].[SAPOrders]
    UNION ALL
    -- Oracle source: renames ItemCount→Quantity, TotalValue→Amount, Area→RegionCode
    SELECT
        OrderID,
        ItemCount      AS Quantity,
        TotalValue     AS Amount,
        Area           AS RegionCode,
        TxDate         AS OrderDate,
        'Oracle' AS SourceSystem
    FROM [ai].[OracleOrders]
